DEMURRER to Amended CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-24-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. RYAN M. ARAKAWA, Esq. of Gaba Law (Tel: 888-391-1228) for plaintiff/cross-defendant Nivoda USA LLC;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: CROSS-DEFENDANT NIVODA USA LLC'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Nivoda USA LLC's ("Nivoda") demurrer to the first amended complaint ("FAC') is sustained with leave to amend in part and overruled in part.
"To prevail on a cause of action for breach of contract, the plaintiff must prove (1) the contract, (2) the plaintiff's performance of the contract or excuse for nonperformance, (3) the defendant's breach, and (4) the resulting damage to the plaintiff." Richman v. Hartley (2014) 224 Cal.App.4th 1182, 1186. If the action is based on alleged breach of a written contract, the terms must be set out verbatim in the body of the complaint or a copy of the written agreement must be attached and incorporated by reference. Harris v. Rudin, Richman & Appel (1999) 74 Cal.App.4th 299, 307.
Nivoda specially and generally demurs to the first cause of action for breach of contract. Nivoda argues that the first amended complaint is uncertain because it fails to state the essential terms of the agreement. MPA 6:25-26. Where a demurrer is made upon uncertainty grounds, it must distinctly specify exactly how or why the pleading is uncertain, and where such uncertainty appears (by reference to page and line numbers of the complaint). See Fenton v. Groveland Comm. Services Dist. (1982) 135 CA3d 797, 809 (disapproved on other grounds by Katzberg v. Regents of Univ. of Calif. (2002) 29 C4th 300, 328, fn. 30). Nivoda fails to reference where the uncertainty appears. See FAC, 7:1. Nonetheless, the written contract was not attached, and the terms of contract were not recited verbatim. Thus, the demurrer is sustained with leave to amend. The Art of Jewels and Fine Art, LLC ("AJFA") must append the contract or recite the terms "verbatim" in the next complaint, which must be filed and served by April 7, 2023.
Negligence
Next, Nivoda argues the second cause of action for negligence must fail because there was no false promise, as alleged under the third cause of action. FAC, 9:11-12. Nivoda also appears to argue that AJFA has not met the standard for tortious breach of contract. (Id. at 9:12:23. Nivoda sufficiently pleaded negligence by alleging duty, breach, causation and damages. FAC, 4:18-26, 5:1-4. Further, the test on general demurrer is whether the complaint states any valid claim, not whether it is the claim plaintiff intended. Quelimane Co. v. Stewart Title Guaranty Co. (1998) 19 Cal.4th 26, 38-39. The demurrer is overruled.
Nivoda fails to raise any argument under CCP 430.10(f).
False Promise
Finally, Nivoda argues that the third cause of action is uncertain because it omits the names of persons who made the alleged representations and the date the representations were made. MPA, 9:2-4. However, the complaint names cross defendant as the person who made promises (FAC, 5:9-10) and lists the date of "on or about March of 2021" as to when the contract was entered into. Id. at 3:1. Nivoda also argues that the complaint is uncertain because it does not specify the manner of the communications. FAC, 9:6-7. Yet, AJFA sufficiently pleads that promises were made in a written contract. FAC, 3:1-4. The demurrer is overruled.
Nivoda fails to raise any argument under CCP 430.10(e).

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which tracks verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Curtis E.A. Karnow; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/CK)
